source: EU AI Act - Provider and Deployer Obligations

provider_obligations.name: Risk Management System
provider_obligations.description: Establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system throughout the AI system's lifecycle.
key_requirements: Identify and analyse known and reasonably foreseeable risks
Estimate and evaluate risks from intended use and reasonably foreseeable misuse
Adopt suitable risk management measures
Test the AI system to identify appropriate risk management measures
Risk management measures shall give due consideration to effects and possible interactions resulting from combined application of requirements
provider_obligations.typical_gap: No formal risk management system exists for the AI use case

provider_obligations.name: Data and Data Governance
provider_obligations.description: Training, validation and testing data sets shall be subject to appropriate data governance and management practices.
key_requirements: Data governance policies covering data collection, preparation, and labeling
Examination for possible biases that are likely to affect health, safety, or fundamental rights
Identification of relevant data gaps or shortcomings
Appropriate statistical properties for the intended purpose
Consideration of geographic, contextual, behavioural, or functional setting
Data sets shall be relevant, sufficiently representative, and to the best extent possible free of errors and complete
provider_obligations.typical_gap: No data governance framework for AI training/evaluation data

provider_obligations.name: Technical Documentation
provider_obligations.description: Draw up technical documentation before the system is placed on the market or put into service, and keep it up to date.
key_requirements: General description of the AI system
Detailed description of elements and development process
Monitoring, functioning and control of the AI system
Description of the risk management system
Description of changes made throughout lifecycle
Must comply with Annex IV content requirements
Technical documentation shall be drawn up in such a way as to demonstrate compliance
provider_obligations.typical_gap: Documentation exists for the model but not formatted per EU AI Act Annex IV requirements

provider_obligations.name: Record-Keeping / Automatic Logging
provider_obligations.description: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for automatic recording of events (logs) throughout the lifetime of the system.
key_requirements: Logging capabilities designed at development stage
Recording of period of each use (start date and time, reference database)
Recording of input data for which the search has led to a match
Identification of natural persons involved in verification of results
Logs shall be appropriate to the intended purpose and in accordance with recognized standards
provider_obligations.typical_gap: System has application logs but not the specific logging required by the Act

provider_obligations.name: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers
provider_obligations.description: Designed and developed to ensure operations are sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret output and use appropriately.
key_requirements: Instructions for use in appropriate digital or non-digital format
Identity and contact details of provider
Characteristics, capabilities and limitations of performance including accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
Changes to the system pre-determined by the provider at assessment time
Human oversight measures including technical measures to facilitate interpretation of outputs
Expected lifetime and necessary maintenance and care measures
Specifications for input data or other relevant information regarding training, validation and testing data sets
provider_obligations.typical_gap: No formal instructions for use document exists

provider_obligations.name: Human Oversight
provider_obligations.description: Designed and developed to be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period of use.
key_requirements: Human oversight measures identified and built in by the provider
Ability to fully understand capacities and limitations of the AI system
Ability to duly monitor operation and detect signs of anomalies, dysfunctions or unexpected performance
Ability to correctly interpret the AI system output
Ability to decide not to use the system or disregard, override or reverse its output
Ability to intervene in or interrupt the system through a stop button or similar procedure
provider_obligations.typical_gap: AI outputs are auto-accepted without human review mechanism

provider_obligations.name: Accuracy, Robustness and Cybersecurity
provider_obligations.description: Designed and developed to achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity and to perform consistently in those respects throughout their lifecycle.
key_requirements: Appropriate level of accuracy declared and in instructions for use
Resilient against errors, faults, or inconsistencies within the system or its environment
Appropriate technical redundancy solutions including backup or fail-safe plans
Protected against unauthorized third-party manipulation of input or training data (data poisoning)
Resilient against attempts to alter use or performance by exploiting system vulnerabilities
Appropriate measures for relevant cybersecurity threats
provider_obligations.typical_gap: No formal accuracy benchmarks or robustness testing documented

provider_obligations.name: Conformity Assessment
provider_obligations.description: Subject to a conformity assessment procedure before being placed on the market or put into service.
key_requirements: Internal conformity assessment per Annex VI for most Annex III categories
Third-party conformity assessment (notified body) required for biometric systems under Annex III Point 1
New conformity assessment required if system is substantially modified
Assessment must be carried out before market placement
provider_obligations.typical_gap: No conformity assessment process exists or planned

provider_obligations.name: EU Declaration of Conformity
provider_obligations.description: Provider draws up a written or electronically signed EU declaration of conformity for each high-risk AI system and keeps it for 10 years after the system has been placed on the market or put into service.
key_requirements: Declaration states system complies with requirements in Chapter III Section 2
Kept up to date
Made available to national competent authorities upon request
Content follows the template in Annex V
Translated into the language required by the Member State

provider_obligations.name: Registration in EU Database
provider_obligations.description: Provider or authorized representative registers the high-risk AI system in the EU database before placing on market or putting into service.
key_requirements: Registration in the EU database referred to in Article 71
Must be done before market placement or putting into service
Deployers of high-risk AI systems must also register their use in the EU database
provider_obligations.typical_gap: No awareness of EU database registration requirement

deployer_obligations.name: Use in Accordance with Instructions
deployer_obligations.description: Use the AI system in accordance with the instructions of use accompanying the system.
key_requirements: Follow provider's instructions for use
Ensure appropriate technical and organisational measures
Ensure input data is relevant to the intended purpose
deployer_obligations.typical_gap: No formal process to review and follow the provider's instructions for use

deployer_obligations.name: Human Oversight
deployer_obligations.description: Assign human oversight to natural persons who have the necessary competence, training and authority.
key_requirements: Designate competent natural persons for human oversight
Ensure those persons have necessary training
Ensure those persons have authority to override or disregard AI output
deployer_obligations.typical_gap: No designated human oversight personnel with documented authority to override AI decisions

deployer_obligations.name: Input Data Relevance
deployer_obligations.description: Ensure that input data is relevant and sufficiently representative in view of the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system, to the extent the deployer exercises control over the input data.
key_requirements: Verify input data relevance for intended purpose
Ensure data is sufficiently representative
Applies to extent the deployer controls input data
deployer_obligations.typical_gap: No process to validate that input data meets the system's requirements for the intended purpose

deployer_obligations.name: Monitoring
deployer_obligations.description: Monitor the operation of the high-risk AI system on the basis of the instructions for use and inform the provider or distributor of any serious incident or malfunctioning.
key_requirements: Monitor operation per instructions for use
Report serious incidents to the provider
Suspend use upon identification of a serious risk
Inform provider/distributor and relevant market surveillance authority of serious incidents
deployer_obligations.typical_gap: No monitoring process aligned with provider instructions; no incident reporting procedure

deployer_obligations.name: Record Keeping
deployer_obligations.description: Keep logs automatically generated by the high-risk AI system, to the extent such logs are under their control, for a period appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system, of at least six months.
key_requirements: Retain automatically generated logs
Minimum retention period of six months
Logs must be under the deployer's control
Retention period must be appropriate to intended purpose and applicable legal obligations
deployer_obligations.typical_gap: Logs not retained or retention period not defined; AI system logs not separated from general application logs

deployer_obligations.name: Inform Workers
deployer_obligations.description: Inform workers' representatives and affected workers that they will be subject to the use of the high-risk AI system before putting it into service or using it in the workplace.
key_requirements: Notify workers' representatives before deployment
Notify affected workers before deployment
Provide information about the AI system's use in the workplace
deployer_obligations.typical_gap: No process to notify workers or worker representatives about AI system deployment

deployer_obligations.name: Data Protection Impact Assessment
deployer_obligations.description: Use information provided under Article 13 to comply with obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment under Article 35 of GDPR or Article 27 of the Law Enforcement Directive.
key_requirements: Carry out DPIA where applicable under GDPR Article 35
Use the provider's Article 13 information as input
Document DPIA results
deployer_obligations.typical_gap: DPIA not conducted or not updated for the AI system use case

deployer_obligations.name: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment
deployer_obligations.description: Before putting a high-risk AI system into use, deployers that are bodies governed by public law or private entities providing public services, and deployers using systems under Annex III points 5(b) and 5(d), shall perform an assessment of the impact on fundamental rights.
key_requirements: Describe the deployer's processes where the AI system will be used
Describe the period of time and frequency in which the AI system will be used
Describe the categories of natural persons and groups likely to be affected
Describe the specific risks of harm likely to impact identified categories of persons or groups
Description of the implementation of human oversight measures
Description of measures to be taken in case of materialization of risks
Notify the market surveillance authority of the results
Must be performed before the system is put into use
applies_to: Deployers using systems under Annex III points 5(b) and 5(d)
deployer_obligations.typical_gap: No fundamental rights impact assessment process exists; organization does not recognize it applies to them

transparency_obligations.applies_to: AI systems that interact with people, generate synthetic content, or perform emotion recognition / biometric categorisation. These apply regardless of risk level.

requirements.trigger: AI system designed to directly interact with natural persons
requirements.obligation: Inform the natural person that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the circumstances and context of use. The information shall be provided in a clear and distinguishable manner at the latest at the time of the first interaction.
requirements.example: Customer service chatbot must disclose it is AI at the start of conversation

requirements.trigger: AI system generates synthetic audio, image, video, or text content
requirements.obligation: Mark the output as artificially generated or manipulated in a machine-readable format. Outputs shall be detectable as artificially generated or manipulated.
requirements.example: AI-generated marketing copy must be labeled; deepfake videos must carry metadata marking

requirements.trigger: Emotion recognition system or biometric categorisation system
requirements.obligation: Inform the natural persons exposed thereto of the operation of the system and process personal data in accordance with GDPR, the Law Enforcement Directive, and other applicable Union law.
requirements.example: Must disclose if scanning faces for emotional response in a retail store

requirements.trigger: AI system generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake
requirements.obligation: Disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated. Does not apply where use is authorised by law for detection, prevention, investigation or prosecution of criminal offences.
requirements.example: A marketing team using AI-generated spokesperson videos must disclose they are AI-generated
